transformation_type: aminomethylation of enolizable carbonyls or other CH-acidic partners with formaldehyde and amines
mechanism_type: iminium-ion formation followed by carbon–carbon bond formation at the alpha position of an enolizable nucleophile
reactant_pattern_text: enolizable carbonyl compound + formaldehyde or other aldehyde + amine
common_conditions: acidic or buffered conditions, sometimes using preformed iminium salts or vinylogous Mannich variants
description_short: Foundational carbon–carbon bond-forming reaction that installs aminomethyl substituents onto enolizable carbonyl systems via iminium intermediates.

transformation_type: low-valent titanium-mediated reductive coupling of carbonyl compounds to alkenes
mechanism_type: reductive dimerization or intramolecular coupling through titanium pinacolate-like intermediates followed by deoxygenation
common_conditions: low-valent titanium generated in situ from titanium chlorides and reducing metals; intra- and intermolecular olefination
description_short: Reductive olefination method that couples carbonyl compounds under low-valent titanium conditions and is especially powerful for macrocyclization.

transformation_type: radical arylation of electron-deficient olefins with aryldiazonium salts
mechanism_type: single-electron reduction of aryldiazonium salts to aryl radicals followed by radical addition and elimination
reactant_pattern_text: substituted aromatic amine + NaNO2/HX -> aryldiazonium salt + activated alkene
common_conditions: in situ diazotization and copper-mediated or thermal radical arylation, often with activated alkenes or dienes
description_short: Diazonium-radical coupling strategy for attaching aryl groups across activated double bonds under simple in situ diazotization conditions.

transformation_type: transfer-hydrogen reduction of aldehydes and ketones with aluminum alkoxides
mechanism_type: concerted hydride transfer through a six-membered aluminum alkoxide transition state
reactant_pattern_text: ketone or aldehyde + secondary alcohol donor
common_conditions: equilibrium transfer-hydrogen reduction often driven by acetone removal or excess donor alcohol
description_short: Mild and chemoselective aluminum-alkoxide transfer-hydrogen reduction of carbonyl compounds to alcohols.

transformation_type: rearrangement of tertiary amine N-oxides to hydroxylamines or ring-expanded heterocycles
mechanism_type: thermal or oxidative [1,2]- and [2,3]-sigmatropic migration from amine N-oxides
common_conditions: N-oxide formation followed by thermal rearrangement or base-promoted capture; cyclic systems often undergo ring expansion
description_short: Rearrangement manifold of tertiary amine N-oxides that furnishes hydroxylamines or ring-expanded azaheterocycles via [1,2]- or [2,3]-shifts.
